title: Roadmap shape thinning post-milestone-elevation — what remains in roadmap when milestones + phases become top-level blocks
description: Per DEC-0024 (milestones-as-anchor enacted) + FGAP-053 (milestone elevation to top-level block) + FGAP-049 (phase pattern divergence; phase elevation likely follows), roadmap.milestones[] inline array + roadmap.phases[] inline array migrate to top-level milestone + phase blocks with closure-table edges. After migration, roadmap retains: id (ROADMAP-NNN), title, description, status (lifecycle: draft / active / paused / complete / archived), created_by, created_at. Substantive structure (which milestones, in what order, gated by which phases) lives in: top-level milestone block + top-level phase block + relations.json edges (relation_type: roadmap_contains_milestone, roadmap_contains_phase, phase_culminates_at_milestone, milestone_depends_on, phase_depends_on) + config.lenses[] composition lens spec for grouping. Roadmap becomes a thin envelope: a named, scoped, lifecycle-bearing curation. Open question about whether roadmap remains a block kind at all OR collapses into config.lenses[] (composition lens binning milestones-by-scope) — collapsing loses per-roadmap lifecycle status, which is the one substantive surface roadmap retains uniquely.

evidence.reference: Current shape: id + title + description + status + phases[] inline + milestones[] inline
FGAP-053 milestone elevation; FGAP-049 phase pattern divergence
loadRoadmap currently reads inline arrays; post-elevation cascades to top-level block loads + edge resolution

impact: Roadmap schema migration + roadmap-plan.ts cascade are downstream of FGAP-053 + FGAP-049 elevation work. If not addressed coherently, roadmap remains carrying inline arrays parallel to elevated blocks, producing two sources of truth + drift potential. Migration touches: schema (roadmap.schema.json revised); registry default (roadmap.json blank instance shape changes); roadmap-plan.ts code (load + render flows); any consumer reading roadmap.phases / roadmap.milestones inline (currently roadmap-plan.ts + tests). Open design questions: (a) does roadmap remain a block kind or collapse into a lens; (b) if it remains, what's the minimum field set; (c) what relation_types canonically connect roadmap to its milestones/phases; (d) how does roadmap-plan render the cascade post-migration.
proposed_resolution: Resolution sequenced after FGAP-053 elevation lands: (1) Revise roadmap.schema.json — drop phases[] + milestones[] inline arrays; retain id + title + description + status + created_by + created_at; consider removing milestones field entirely OR repurposing as a backlog of ad-hoc-attached milestones not modeled by the canonical structure. (2) Author relation_types in config.relation_types[] — roadmap_contains_milestone (membership), roadmap_contains_phase (membership), phase_culminates_at_milestone (membership). (3) Update roadmap-plan.ts loadRoadmap to resolve milestones + phases via top-level block loads + edge traversals rather than inline. (4) Optional: define a composition lens (config.lenses[]) per roadmap to project member items via groupByLens; lens definition lives in config.lenses[], roadmap.id ties to lens.id. (5) Cross-decision with FGAP-053 + FGAP-049: this resolution lands AFTER both elevation arcs complete. (6) Could also evaluate whether roadmap collapses entirely into a lens — tradeoff: loses per-roadmap lifecycle status which is the unique surface roadmap retains; recommend keeping roadmap as thin block.
